title: AI Pair Programming — The Copilot Awakens

steps.title: Welcome to the Lab
steps.instruction: Welcome! In this hands-on lab you'll set up GitHub Copilot and experience inline completions and Copilot Chat first-hand. Follow the steps in this panel — each one walks you through a real interaction.

steps.title: Install the Extension
steps.instruction: Open the Extensions sidebar (Ctrl+Shift+X), search for GitHub Copilot, and click Install. A single extension now bundles both inline completions and Copilot Chat.

steps.title: Sign In to GitHub
steps.instruction: Click the Copilot icon in the status bar → Sign in to GitHub. Authorize VS Code in your browser, then come back here. The icon should turn into the Copilot sparkle when you're active.

steps.title: Choose Your Model
steps.instruction: Click the model selector dropdown to see available models. Different models have different strengths — Claude excels at reasoning, GPT-4o is fast and versatile. For this lab, any model works.

steps.title: Your First Prompt
steps.instruction: Type a question in the chat input. Try asking: <kbd>What is GitHub Copilot and how does it help developers?</kbd>
steps.inputValue: What is GitHub Copilot and how does it help developers?

steps.title: Read the Response
steps.instruction: Copilot responds with a clear, structured explanation. Notice how it uses code formatting, bullet points, and practical examples.

messages.content: What is GitHub Copilot and how does it help developers?

GitHub Copilot is an AI-powered coding assistant that integrates directly into your editor. Here's what it does:

Inline Completions — As you type, Copilot suggests whole lines or entire functions as grey "ghost text." Press Tab to accept.

Chat — Ask questions in natural language, get explanations, generate code, and refactor existing work.

Context-Aware — Copilot reads your open files, comments, and function signatures to give relevant suggestions.

Think of it as a pair programmer that never gets tired and has read billions of lines of code. 🚀

steps.title: Try Inline Completions
steps.instruction:
Open a new file (Ctrl+N), set the language to JavaScript, and type this comment:

// Calculate the total price of items in a shopping cart

Then press Enter and watch Copilot suggest the function body as ghost text. Press Tab to accept.

messages.content: What is GitHub Copilot and how does it help developers?
GitHub Copilot is an AI-powered coding assistant that integrates directly into your editor. It provides inline completions and conversational chat to help you code faster.

steps.title: Ask for an Explanation
steps.instruction: Select the function you just generated, then type in chat: <kbd>/explain</kbd>. Copilot will break down how the code works step by step.

messages.content: What is GitHub Copilot and how does it help developers?
GitHub Copilot is an AI-powered coding assistant that integrates directly into your editor.
/explain

steps.title: Ask for a Fix
steps.instruction: Now try <kbd>/fix</kbd> — imagine your function doesn't handle empty arrays or missing prices. Copilot will suggest defensive checks.

messages.content: /explain
Your calculateTotal function uses .reduce() to sum up price × quantity for each item.
/fix

steps.title: Generate Tests
steps.instruction: Select your function and type <kbd>/tests</kbd>. Copilot will generate unit tests covering normal cases, edge cases, and error conditions.

messages.content: /fix
I improved the function with guard clauses and type coercion.
/tests

steps.title: Configure Copilot Settings
steps.instruction: Open your settings (Ctrl+,) and search for copilot. Try toggling github.copilot.enable per language — for example, disable Copilot for Markdown but keep it on for JavaScript and Python.

messages.content: /tests
Generated 5 unit tests covering normal cases, edge cases, and type coercion.
How do I configure Copilot to only work in certain languages?

steps.title: Lab Complete!
steps.instruction: Congratulations! You've activated Copilot, used inline completions, explored Chat commands (/explain, /fix, /tests), and configured workspace settings. You're ready for the next course: MCP for You and Me.

messages.content: Thanks Copilot! What should I learn next? 🎉

Great job completing the lab! Here's your roadmap:

🔹 MCP for You and Me — Plug in GitHub's MCP server and manage issues without leaving VS Code
🔹 Prompts, Instructions, Skills, and Agents — Master the four building blocks of Copilot customization
🔹 Agent Potato: License to Chat — Build a full-stack app with Copilot as your AI pair programmer

Quick reference for what you learned today:
• Tab — Accept inline suggestion
• Ctrl+I — Inline chat
• Ctrl+Shift+I — Open chat panel
• /explain /fix /tests — Chat commands
• @workspace — Project-wide questions

Happy coding! 🚀
